CERTIFICATION OF PASSAGE OF AN ACT TO 1MAGA
This is to certify that Bill No. 14-38 (COR), "AN ACT TO AMEND 1909(c) OF
CHAPTER 19, TITLE 1, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO THE
DUTIES OF THE PUBLIC AUDITOR," was reconsidered by 1 Liheslaturan
Guåhan and after such consideration, did agree, on the Yd day of June 2025, to pass
said bill notwithstandingthe objections oflMaga'hågan Guåhan by avoteoftwelve
(12) members.

AN ACT TO AMEND 1909(c) OF CHAPTER 19, TITLE
1, GUAM CODE ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO THE
DUTIES OF THE PUBLIC AUDITOR.
1 BE IT ENACTED BY THE PEOPLE OF GUAM:
2 Section 1. 1909(c) of Chapter 19, Title l, Guam Code Annotated, is
3 hereby amendedto read:
4 "(c) To communicate directly with any person or with any
5 department, officer or person having official relations with the office in any
6 matter relating to the expenditures ofgovernment funds and property or to the
7 settlement thereof; and
1
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0733.*

1 (1) establish deadlines for submission by every department or
2 agency head, inclusive of autonomous entities, or head of a program
3 withinthe government ofGuam ofall financial or program information
4 under their control as are necessary to carry outthe duties and functions
5 of the Office of Public Accountability. Failure to submit such
6 information by the required deadline shall result in the imposition of a
7 Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ($250.00) fine against the general manager,
8 director, or head of the agency, in their personal capacity, per missed
9 deadline which shall be deposited into the Guam Memorial Hospital
10 Operations Fund. An additional fine of Two Hundred Fifty Dollars
11 ($250.00) shall be assessed against the general manager, director, or
12 head ofthe agency, in their personal capacity, for every thirty (30) days
13 that the reporting requirements are not met.
14 (2) The Public Auditor shall file a report of those general
15 managers, directors, or heads of the agency who fail to pay the fine
16 imposed herein within thirty (30) days with the Office of the Attorney
17 General ofGuam for enforcement."
18 (3) Deadline Extension.
19 (a) Notwithstanding the deadlines set forth within this
20 Act, an agency director who is unable to comply with the
21 submission deadline specified herein may submit a written
22 request to the Office of Public Accountability (OPA) for an
23 extension at least five (5) days prior to the established deadline.
24 The request must clearly specify the reasons for noncompliance
25 and propose an alternative submission date.
26 (b) Upon receipt ofthe request, the OPA shall issue a
27 written decision within three (3) business days. Should the OPA
2
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0733.*

1 grant the extension, the decision shall clearly indicate the revised
2 deadline. If the extension is denied, the written decision shall
3 specify the reasons for denial and must be transmitted to I
4 Maga 'hågan/Maga 'låhen Guåhan and the Speaker of I
5 Liheslaturan Guåhan within forty-eight (48) hours of issuance."
3
Doc. No. 38GL-25-0733.*
